title: Off-Policy Corrected Reward Modeling for Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
problem: Overoptimization or Goodharting in RLHF where the reward model becomes inaccurate as the RL model diverges from the SFT model
solution: Off-Policy Corrected Reward Modeling (OCRM) using importance weighting (IW) to correct for distribution shift

method.implementation: Multiple training stages, each consisting of training a corrected RM with IW and then performing multiple policy updates

key_findings: Standard RLHF methods provide inconsistent estimates of RM parameters
Standard RLHF generally doesn't converge to optimal policy
OCRM iteratively improves RM accuracy and converges to higher final reward
visualization: Figure 1 shows visualization of the approach in a 2D stateless task, comparing true reward functions, learned RMs, SFT policy, and learned policy distributions
